sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano are struggling, with no wins in their last 4 matches and a tendency to concede first. Sevilla have shown mixed results, with 2 wins and 3 losses in their last 5, but they often see both teams scoring in their games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last 2 head-to-head matches, results have been close, with a 1-1 draw and a 1-0 Sevilla win. Matches between these teams often end with under 2.5 goals and both teams scoring.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano (14th, 5 points) have scored 7 goals but struggle defensively with no clean sheets. Sevilla (12th, 7 points) have a balanced attack and defense (10 goals scored and conceded) but also lack clean sheets recently.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Trends
sections.text: Rayo’s Jorge de Frutos and Álvaro García are in form, contributing to recent goals. Sevilla’s ability to score in crucial periods (46-60 minutes) could exploit Rayo’s defensive lapses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, the score remains goalless between Rayo Vallecano and Sevilla at the Estadio de Vallecas in Madrid. Rayo Vallecano have dominated possession and created more threatening opportunities, evident from their higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area. Sevilla, on the other hand, have struggled to make an impact offensively, failing to register a shot on target in the first half. Both teams have been cautious, with minimal disciplinary issues, as only one yellow card has been shown so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano have shown clear intent to control the game, holding a significant share of the ball and consistently pushing into Sevilla’s defensive third. Their ability to win duels has been balanced, and they’ve earned several corner kicks to apply pressure. Players like Álvaro García and Florian Lejeune have been involved in dangerous moments, with shots on target hinting at potential breakthroughs. However, they’ve yet to convert their dominance into goals, which could be a concern if they fail to capitalize in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Sevilla's first half performance
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla have been on the back foot for much of the first half, with limited possession and fewer entries into Rayo’s final third. Their attacking output has been minimal, with no shots on target and a lack of presence in key areas. Defensively, they’ve held firm, relying on clearances and aerial strength to thwart Rayo’s advances. Players like José Ángel Carmona and Fábio Cardoso have contributed to keeping a clean sheet so far, but their lack of offensive threat could leave them vulnerable if Rayo maintain their pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half swung heavily in favor of Rayo Vallecano for extended periods, with consistent spells of attacking intent, especially in the latter stages of the half. They mounted several waves of pressure, particularly towards the end, showcasing their determination to break the deadlock. Sevilla had brief moments of forward push, mainly in short bursts during the middle of the half, but failed to sustain any significant threat. This disparity suggests Rayo are more likely to carry their attacking rhythm into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Rayo Vallecano, Álvaro García stands out as a potential game-changer in the second half, having already tested Sevilla’s defense with a shot on target. His involvement in attacking plays could be crucial. On the Sevilla side, Isaac Romero might be the one to spark a counterattack, given his potential threat in limited opportunities. Both teams will likely look to these players to unlock the game, with Rayo having the edge based on first-half contributions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the trends from the first half, Rayo Vallecano are likely to continue their dominance in possession and push for a breakthrough. Their ability to create chances in the final third should eventually yield results if they maintain intensity. Sevilla will need to focus on counterattacks or set pieces to exploit any lapses in Rayo’s defense, as their direct attacking play has been lacking. Fatigue could play a factor, but Rayo’s home advantage at Estadio de Vallecas may give them the edge to secure a narrow lead.
